Ir is evident that] our recent appeal for a
forward move in connection with the Wolfe
Tone Memorial Tournament has borne
fruit. At a meeting of the Leinster Coun-

offer the services of the Council to organise
and promote the tournament so far as
Leinster was concerned, and it was

mised that the competition would be well
under weigh in the New Year. The
object aimed at by the meeting is to relieve
the Central Council of the task of carrying
out the initial rounds of the competition,
and leaving them only the semi-finals
finals to decide,

Leinster Council's idea is that the other
Provincial Councils should stmilarly take
charge of the earlier rounds within their
‘own provinces, and, by a process of elimina.
tion, that they should send forward the best
hurling and football counties under their
control to contest the semi-finals.
